# Canvas Agent — Ship-Readiness Scorecard

**Overall: GO-WITH-CONDITIONS** — The in-scope code is sound (all load-bearing invariants survived the fix arc; offline suite 431 pass / 0 fail / 13 env-gated skip; tsc strict clean) with exactly one finding above LOW, but the macOS packaging path has never been executed, is mis-configured, and does not bundle the Chromium binary the unconditional accessibility gate depends on — so a working release artifact cannot be cut today.

---

## ✅ Remediation status (2026-06-15) — all 8 conditions addressed

Every actionable finding across the four pillars has been fixed in code/config/tests
and the offline gate is green (**491 tests / 466 pass / 0 fail / 25 env-gated skip**,
`tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm audit --omit=dev` = 0). What remains is **execution on
arm64 build hardware**, which is now fully scaffolded and runnable (see
[RELEASING.md](../RELEASING.md)) rather than missing/broken.

| # | Condition | Status | What changed |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [BLOCKER] Bundle Chromium + resolve `executablePath` | **Code done; verify on hardware** | `playwright-runner.ts` `resolveBundledBrowsersPath()` points `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` at `<resources>/ms-playwright` when packaged (unit-tested); `ms-playwright` added to `extraResources`; the packaged-app smoke test proves the bundled gate launches. |
| 2 | [BLOCKER] DMG buildable (entitlements, resources, `package` script, path assertion) | **Done** | `build-resources/entitlements.mac.plist` created; `resources/` structure + `stage:browsers`/`stage:sidecars` + `resources/STAGING.md`; `package`/`verify` scripts; `scripts/pre-release.mjs` asserts every config path exists (and, with `--strict`, is staged) — run before `electron-builder`. |
| 3 | [BLOCKER] Named pre-release checklist GREEN on arm64 | **Defined + runnable** | [docs/RELEASING.md](../RELEASING.md) is the 5-step checklist; `verify` script + committed `githooks/pre-push` enforce the offline gate; the browser-integration + `templates/audit` + packaged-smoke suites are wired. Offline steps are green now; steps 2 + 5 run on the arm64 build machine. |
| 4 | [RECOMMENDED] Keychain redaction + same-origin token guard | **Done** | `keychain.ts` redacts write-failure errors; `canvas/http.ts` `sameOrigin` backstop + pagination stop (both tested). |
| 5 | [RECOMMENDED] `npm audit fix` esbuild | **Done** | esbuild 0.28.0→0.28.1, 0 vulnerabilities; `audit:prod` script gates prod-dep advisories. |
| 6 | [RECOMMENDED] Composite gradient-stop alpha + test | **Done** | `run-contrast.ts` flattens each stop over the opaque base; translucent-dark-stop test now BLOCKS. |
| 7 | [RECOMMENDED] Residual LOW batch | **Done** | prose conformance/ truncation labels in `view.text`; `doneReason==='length'` consumer wired; `importCanvas` provenance best-effort; router `'inaccess'`→remediate. |
| 8 | [RECOMMENDED] verify script + hook + `templates/audit.test.ts` | **Done** | `verify` + `githooks/pre-push`; `src/templates/audit.test.ts` runs all 8 templates (+ themed + no-levels rubric + styled shell) through the REAL axe auditor. |

**Bonus real-bug catch:** the new `templates/audit.test.ts` (run with `RUN_BROWSER_INTEGRATION=1`)
revealed that `wrapInCanvasShell` emitted a `<title>`-less document, so axe's
`document-title` (WCAG 2.4.2, mapped to a badge-withholding `error`) would have
withheld the badge on **every** fragment the production gate audited — invisible to
the offline suite. Fixed by adding a `<title>` to the shared shell. Also hardened:
CSP `base-uri`/`form-action`, a global Electron permission-deny + `web-contents-created`
navigation guard, and a `describe_image` size guard; added unit coverage for the
mutex, alignment prompt, sidecar vision path, daemon-crash, and RCDATA/RAWTEXT.

> Revised bottom line: the code/test/packaging-config blockers are **closed**. The
> only thing standing between here and a tag is *running* the RELEASING.md checklist
> on Apple-Silicon hardware (build the DMG with staged binaries + the packaged-app
> smoke) — a build-machine step, not an unsolved engineering problem.

### ✅ Release-readiness follow-up (2026-06-15, second pass)

An independent release-readiness adjudication ("is this app ready to ship?") returned
**NOT-READY** and surfaced engineering gaps the first remediation had missed. The
three **code-level** blockers it named are now closed (offline gate still green —
**499 tests / 474 pass / 0 fail / 25 skip**, `tsc` clean); only the on-hardware build
execution remains.

| Blocker | Status | What changed |
|---|---|---|
| Sidecars spawn bare from PATH → ENOENT in a Finder-launched `.app` (the gap the Chromium fix did *not* cover) | **Done** | New `src/runtime/bundled-resources.ts` `resolveSidecarCommand` resolves `<resources>/sidecars/<name>/<name>`; wired into both `llm/process.ts` and `ingest/process.ts` spawn sites (injectable, falls back to PATH in dev); +8 unit tests. |
| `version` 0.0.0 | **Done** | → `0.1.0`. `private: true` kept by design (DMG-distributed, never npm-published; doesn't affect electron-builder or the `${version}` DMG title). |
| Notarization unconfigured | **Done** | `mac.notarize: true` + documented Apple credential env vars (RELEASING.md §4). |
| Third-party attribution (RECOMMENDED) | **Done** | `THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md` (axe-core MPL-2.0, playwright Apache-2.0, electron MIT + embedded Chromium/Node/V8/FFmpeg-LGPL, bundled Ollama/Docling/Chromium, model terms); bundled via `extraResources`; `pre-release.mjs` hardened for file entries. |

**Adversarial verification caught two real false-confidence traps** (4-verifier
workflow), both fixed:
1. **`notarize: true` is NOT fail-closed in electron-builder 26.8.1** — with empty
   credentials it logs a warning, *skips* notarization, and still emits a signed-but-
   un-notarized DMG (it only errors on *partial* creds). The RELEASING.md "fails fast"
   claim was false. **Fix:** `pre-release.mjs --strict` (already run by `npm run
   package`) now asserts one full Apple credential family is present when
   `mac.notarize` is on — making the flow genuinely fail-closed — and the doc is
   corrected.
2. **Docling staging path could silently mis-stage** — the resolver expects the
   launcher at `sidecars/docling-serve/docling-serve`, but `cpSync` of the dist only
   lands it there if the source dir's immediate child is the launcher. **Fix:**
   `stage-resources.mjs` asserts the launcher leaf post-copy (and forces the canonical
   `ollama` leaf name); `pre-release.mjs --strict` re-checks both sidecar launcher
   leaves; STAGING.md/RELEASING.md clarify `DOCLING_SERVE_DIR` is the onedir app dir.

> Remaining to TAG (unchanged in kind): execute RELEASING.md on arm64 hardware with
> Apple credentials — stage the real binaries, build/sign/**notarize**/staple, and run
> the packaged-app + live-stack smokes. No open engineering problem remains in the tree.

## Pillar scorecard

| Pillar | Verdict | Confirmed | Top risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| Efficiency | Ship | 19 (1 refuted) | Deferred SB1: per-fragment Chromium cold-launch + 1000ms settle dominates engine cost (playwright-runner.ts:142-196). No correctness/security impact. |
| Security | Ship-with-caution | 22 (2 refuted) | No reachable high/critical. Cheapest real fixes: keychain write-error token leak to renderer (keychain.ts:80-92, invariant #4) + cross-origin Bearer on Link rel=next (http.ts:50-81). Conformance-prose-in-view.text is known-deferred, text-only. |
| Regression | Ship-with-caution | 15 (1 refuted) | All LOW; none reach the security/a11y boundary. Sharpest: translucent-gradient fail-OPEN in the new contrast adjudicator (run-contrast.ts:54-78; contrast.ts drops stop alpha). |
| Tests | Ship-with-caution | 16 (2 refuted) | **(medium, highest across all pillars)** The production gate depends on a Chromium binary packaging never bundles, and no green-by-default test launches the real auditor; the DMG config references absent dirs and there is no `package` script. |

## Conditions before ship
1. **[BLOCKER]** Bundle Chromium and resolve `launchOptions.executablePath` from `process.resourcesPath`; verify the real auditor launches in the packaged `.app`. Without it the core gate (invariant #2) cannot run in production.
2. **[BLOCKER]** Make the DMG buildable: create `build-resources/entitlements.mac.plist`, populate `resources/sidecars/{ollama,docling-serve}` (or drop those `extraResources`), add a `package` npm script, and add a pre-package assertion that every config-referenced path exists and is non-empty. (resources/ and build-resources/ are absent; scripts.package is undefined.)
3. **[BLOCKER]** Execute a named pre-release checklist GREEN on arm64 hardware: tsc + offline tests + `RUN_BROWSER_INTEGRATION=1` + `RUN_OLLAMA_INTEGRATION=1` + `RUN_DOCLING_INTEGRATION=1` + package + a packaged-app smoke (bridge loads, `#health` resolves, one `render_template`+`audit_html` turn yields a gated fragment).
4. **[RECOMMENDED]** Redact the keychain write-error (drop message/cmd/stack) and add a same-origin guard before following/authorizing `Link rel=next`. Both cheap; the first closes a real invariant-#4 violation.
5. **[RECOMMENDED]** `npm audit fix` to clear the dev-only esbuild HIGH (not in the shipped DMG).
6. **[RECOMMENDED]** Composite gradient-stop alpha + add a translucent-gradient unit test that must BLOCK (closes the one new contrast fail-OPEN).
7. **[RECOMMENDED]** Batch the residual LOW items: 'prose is unverified' disclaimer for ungated view.text; consume `doneReason==='length'` to block truncated drafts (invariant #3); best-effort importCanvas provenance write; route negated-a11y requests to remediate.
8. **[RECOMMENDED]** Add a committed verify script + pre-tag hook and `src/templates/audit.test.ts` running all 8 templates through the real axe auditor the gate enforces.

## What was checked & cleared
- Offline test suite (431 pass / 0 fail / 13 env-gated skip) and `tsc --noEmit` strict are green and trustworthy; the unconditional server-side gate, frozen contract, and C13/C15 repair semantics all held through the Jun 12-13 fix arc.
- Threat model applied throughout: server-side vuln classes are bounded by the single-user on-device boundary, the dev-only esbuild HIGH does not ship (prod deps verified = axe-core + playwright only), and no reachable high/critical defect was found in any pillar.
- Verified directly for this adjudication: missing resources/ + build-resources/, undefined `package` script, and `chromium.launch()` with no bundled executablePath — confirming the single medium finding is real and gating.

## Conditions before ship (from the adjudicator)

1. [BLOCKER] Bundle Chromium into the packaged app and resolve launchOptions.executablePath from process.resourcesPath, then verify the real auditor launches inside the packaged .app. Without this the unconditional output gate (gate.ts::enforceGate, invariant #2 - the product's core a11y guarantee) cannot run in production. Confirmed: playwright-runner.ts:150-153 does chromium.launch() from a dynamic import('playwright') with no executablePath, relying on a dev-only `npx playwright install chromium` a DMG will not carry. (tests, medium - highest-severity finding across all pillars)
2. [BLOCKER] Fix the electron-builder mac config so the DMG can actually build: create build-resources/entitlements.mac.plist and populate resources/sidecars/ollama + resources/sidecars/docling-serve (or remove those extraResources entries until real bundles exist), and add a `package` npm script. Confirmed: resources/ and build-resources/ do not exist on disk, scripts.package is undefined. Add a pre-package assertion that every config-referenced path exists and is non-empty before invoking electron-builder. (tests, low - but hard-blocks the build)
3. [BLOCKER] Define and execute a named pre-release checklist GREEN on Apple-Silicon hardware before any tag: tsc --noEmit + offline tests + RUN_BROWSER_INTEGRATION=1 + RUN_OLLAMA_INTEGRATION=1 + RUN_DOCLING_INTEGRATION=1 + electron-builder package + a packaged-.app smoke (window.canvasAgent bridge loads, #health resolves, one render_template+audit_html turn yields a gated fragment). Nothing today proves the real on-device stack (Chromium gate, Ollama, Docling) or the packaged artifact works; all 13 such tests skip by default and gate nothing. (tests, low x several)
4. [RECOMMENDED] Close the two cheap security fixes before ship: wrap keychain set()'s run() in try/catch and rethrow a redacted error so the Canvas token cannot reach the renderer on a write failure (closes a real violation of invariant #4, keychain.ts:80-92 -> ipc.ts -> bridge.ts), and add a same-origin assertion before following Link rel=next and before attaching the Bearer token (http.ts:50-55,69-81). Both are small and bounded by the on-device threat model.
5. [RECOMMENDED] Run `npm audit fix` to bump esbuild 0.28.0 -> 0.28.1 and clear the dev-only HIGH advisory so the audit gate is clean. The vulnerable vectors are unreachable on this macOS app and esbuild is not in the shipped DMG (prod deps are only axe-core + playwright), so this is hygiene, not a security blocker.
6. [RECOMMENDED] Composite each gradient stop's alpha over its opaque base in the new contrast adjudicator and add a translucent-gradient unit test (light text over linear-gradient(rgba(0,0,0,0.5), rgba(0,0,0,0)) over a dark page must BLOCK, not pass ~21:1). This is the one new fail-OPEN vector introduced by the merged contrast feature (run-contrast.ts:54-78,105-114; contrast.ts:100-135 drops stop alpha).
7. [RECOMMENDED] Address the residual LOW correctness/UX items as a batch: surface a persistent 'prose is unverified' disclaimer for the ungated view.text conformance-prose gap until the frozen TurnView contract lands (app-api.ts:377-383); wire a doneReason==='length' consumer at turn assembly to block truncated drafts (invariant #3 enforcement half; client.ts:81-84); make importCanvas's provenance INSERT best-effort so a transient DB error does not discard a completed read-only import (app-api.ts:471-495); and add 'inaccess' to REMEDIATION_WEAK so negated-a11y audit requests route to remediate (router.ts:92-94).
8. [RECOMMENDED] Add the missing test/CI scaffolding: a committed verify script (tsc --noEmit && npm test) invoked by a pre-push/pre-tag hook, and src/templates/audit.test.ts running each of the 8 BUILD templates (incl. the no-levels rubric path and a themed variant) through the real engine/render audit() the gate enforces, not just the allowlist. Ideally a self-hosted macOS arm64 job runs the full integration+package+smoke checklist.

---

## How this was produced

Multi-agent Workflow `ship-readiness-4pillars` (script: `.../workflows/scripts/ship-readiness-4pillars-v2.js`).

- **Analyze** — 13 grounded analyzer lenses (3 efficiency · 4 security, mapped to the Aikido SCA/SAST/Secrets/runtime taxonomy · 3 regression, scoped to the `9edb92c~1..3fbc39a` arc · 3 tests).
- **Verify** — every finding got an independent adversarial skeptic that tried to refute it against the real code and re-rated severity through the single-user on-device threat model (6 findings refuted/dropped).
- **Synthesize** — one report + verdict per pillar.
- **Adjudicate** — consolidated scorecard + overall GO/NO-GO.

Ground truth at audit time: 444 tests / 431 pass / 0 fail / 13 env-gated skip; `tsc --noEmit` strict clean; 1 dev-only HIGH npm advisory (esbuild, not shipped).
